review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: It is a good pay master at the initial stage of the career. It gives an elite feel in training period of one year.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
The major setback is both learning & growth are stagnant. Unlike in private sector, one can not get exponential pay hikes. Hardly, on an average growth in earnings is not more than 5% in an year. There is no recognition for talent & hard work. Time based promotion system does not give any motivation to work. Promotion system is also not transparent.  Performance management system is rudimentary system which needs lot of improvements. 

Another setback is location of posting. All plants are spread over in rural areas of INDIA. One will feel disconnection with rest of the world. Forget about exposure to outside world.

There is no work life balance. It is unimaginable to have a working spouse. The place where you get posted is not less than a forest, spouses will not get any opportunities in forests.

Transfer policy is not transparent. Those who have political links, easily gets transfers to dream locations. Those who do not have political links, hardly get any opportunity for transfers.

It is impossible to get good health care & education facilities. Thanks to rural setups.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: It is indisputable that there can be no comparison of compensation between the public and private sector but the salaries at this organization are good enough for one to lead a comfortable and fulfilling life. The medical benefits are a great advantage, particularly when they also cover one's dependents (children, parents etc). The work is pretty chilled out, not only because it is a PSU but also because the organization is very systems driven and there is a process for almost everything that one can imagine. The counterparts in the private sector misconstrue this as red-tape but red-tape is a separate matter. The systems driven approach actually makes working very organized. This is in my limited experience as a deputy manager at the Noida office. People posted in the plant may offer a completely opposed view.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: There is unnecessary friction between the employees posted at the plant, which are usually very remote areas and the corporate office/regional offices, which are usually in good cities such as Delhi, Mumbai, Raipur etc. Usually, poeple tend to move away from the plant sites or are averse to such postings since it weighs against their children's education. Lack of sophisticated healthcare is another issue why people are shy of plant postings. Having said that, a major chunk of the strength resides in the projects. Being a bulky organization, the approach to any problem is largely top-down. Growth is painfully slow and responsibilities are delegated only after one has spent considerable time in the organization. This limits the professional growth of esp in early stages of career. These handicaps are, however, common to the public sector in general.

Also they have the policy of placing them in shift pattern for initial 15-20 years of service. Also there is no transparent job rotation and transfer policy.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Overall the job is good, but most of the deserving employees suffer from an Identity Crisis.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
1.Very good salary and benefits especially medical facilities.
2.Work life balance is cool.
3.Plenty of time and money to enjoy with your family.
4.If you are lucky and get posted in design, then lot of opportunities and foreign training.
5.Average quality of people surrounding you is good.
6.No feudal culture like in other government organisation.  
7.Very good 'resale' value.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1.No policy of postings and transfers.
2.A 'jugad' in some ministry or higher management can get u anything in NTPC.
3.No policy of job rotation, so whole of your career is decided by your first posting.
4.Most of the field postings are very remote with no compensatory facilities.
5.No motivation for extra effort.
6.No transparency in promotions.
7.HR and Finance people can harass executives anytime.
8.Nature of job is such that u end up being an employee and not a manager.
9.Most of the talented engineers are frustrated because of no recognition.
10.Not extra benefit for people who have to do night shifts.
11.Very few female colleagues. 
12.Overall work culture is very "nobody is bothered kind-of" so u are never looked at like a responsible executive.
